races.race_name: Get Best Odds With Oddschecker Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Queen Of Christmas
runners.spotlight: 7-2 when fifth of ten on debut at Nottingham (5f, good) three weeks ago, beaten just over 4l by the winner Senorita Bonita who was runner-up at Royal Ascot last week; this 305,000euros yearling has an attractive pedigree and is open to improvement for a top trainer who won this last year with one taking a big step forward on her second start.
runners.comment: Only 5th at Nottingham on debut but this 305,000euros yearling may take a big step forward

runners.name: By Jove Grace
runners.spotlight: 1,000euros yearling, resold 25,000euros yearling, £70,000 2yo breeze-ups; third foal; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 69), half-sister to some talented winners notably Mac Love (7f-1m2f Group 3) and Club Oceanic (useful 1m-1m2f); this Inns Of Court newcomer could give a good account.
runners.comment: £70,000 2yo breeze-ups; dam half-sister to some talented winners; could give good account
runners.owner: Mpr, A & Glo Outlaw & K Quinn

runners.name: In The Black
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns foal, 320,000gns yearling; eighth foal; Havana Grey sister to two winners including Symbology (Group-placed 6f 2yo), closely related to 5f-7f winner El Caballo (including 2yo/Group 2), half-sister to two Italian 2yo winners; dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 91); bred to be a classy juvenile and she has to be of interest on debut.
runners.comment: 320,000gns yearling; bred to be a classy juvenile and she has to be of interest on debut

runners.name: Louis The Fifth
runners.spotlight: 90,000gns yearling, 215,000euros 2yo breeze-ups; eighth foal; No Nay Never brother to winners Noble Vow (5f/5.7f; RPR 81) and Angelica Bay (US 1m turf); dam 6.5f and 1m (Listed) 2yo winner; he's travelled from Newmarket to make his debut and could have a big part to play.
runners.comment: 215,000euros 2yo breeze-ups; this Newmarket raider could have a big part to play on debut

runners.name: Sod Hall Lane
runners.spotlight: 95,000euros yearling; second foal; Blue Point half-brother to Italian 6f/7.5f 2yo winner Moonage Daydream; dam runner-up 7f 2yo (RPR 79), sister to 7f Listed winner Fadhayyil, half-sister to two Listed winners, out of 5f Group 3 winner; could go well on debut, especially if backed.
runners.comment: 95,000euros yearling; dam from useful family; could go well on debut, especially if backed

races.race_name: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Silesia
runners.spotlight: Stepped it up on all three starts over 5f, culminating with solid third of eight in the Hilary Needler at Beverley (good to firm) 25 days ago; that wasn't the strongest renewal of that race but 6f should be no problem and she sets a clear standard; the one to beat.
runners.comment: Stepped it up all three starts over 5f; 6f should be no problem and sets a clear standard

runners.name: Enhancing
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling by Too Darn Hot; 8-1 when eighth of 12 at Newbury (6.5f, good to firm) on her introduction three weeks ago; that form's received a few boosts, however; tongue-tie goes on for her second start.
runners.comment: 8-1 when eighth on debut but that form's received boosts; tongue-tie goes on; interesting

runners.name: Independent Roze
runners.spotlight: By Bangkok; second foal; dam 6f winner (RPR 78), half-sister to winners Bear Faced (7f AW), Mercenary Rose (5f AW including 2yo) and Munificent (5f including AW), out of useful 6f-8.6f AW winner; not ruled out on pedigree but yard is 0-39 with juveniles over the last five years.
runners.comment: By Bangkok; not ruled out on pedigree but yard is 0-39 with juveniles over last five years

runners.name: Love Tanya
runners.spotlight: 110,000euros yearling by Blue Point; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Soldier's Empire (1m/1m2f including 2yo/AW/Bahrain; RPR 96), Mariesque (9.4f; 94), Green Soul (7f 2yo; 83) and Green Backspin (Scandinavian 6f-1m1f); dam French 7f (Listed) and 1m winner (99); respected yard among the winners and she needs monitoring in the market ahead of her debut.
runners.comment: 110,000euros yearling by Blue Point; respected yard and she needs monitoring in the market

runners.name: One Shot Kodi
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros foal by Kodi Bear; 33-1 when prominent before weakening to beat one home at Redcar (6f, good to firm) on her debut four weeks ago; will need a sizeable step forward and yard holds much better prospects with Silesia.
runners.comment: 16,000euros foal by Kodi Bear; 33-1 when beating one home (6f, good to firm) on her debut

runners.name: Ruby Moon
runners.spotlight: By No Nay Never; third foal; dam unplaced, half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Midnight Mile, out of maiden close relative of Irish 1,000 Guineas winner Yesterday and Moyglare Stud winner Quarter Moon (both runner-up in Oaks); yard hasn't been firing an all cylinders with its juveniles but did have one run a screamer at Royal Ascot last week; betting can guide.
runners.comment: Family of G1 winners Yesterday & Quarter Moon (both runner-up in Oaks); likely debutante

races.race_name: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Social Spirit
runners.spotlight: He rallied for third of nine in Doncaster maiden (5f, good to firm) in May and it was a solid effort up at 6f when 25-1 and fifth of 13 in the Woodcote at Epsom (good to soft) on Oaks day; already sets the standard and this new trip promises to unlock more.
runners.comment: Promising debut and solid effort at Epsom latest; sets the standard and new trip can suit

runners.name: Ricky Punting
runners.spotlight: Promise at Bath (firm) on his April debut and again when second at Hamilton (6f, good) in May; wheels came off as favourite next time but that was only one week later on good to soft ground; cheekpieces go on and he could bounce back.
runners.comment: Promise before disappointing latest; excuses there & cheekpieces go on; could bounce back

runners.name: Mia Fantasia
runners.spotlight: Make Believe colt; 40,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Princess Mia (1m; RPR 67) and Wishforluck (Swedish 6f/7f including 2yo); dam French 1m4f winner, half-sister to winners Secret Existence (10.6f) and Twenty Times (1m2f), out of 1m2f/10.6f winner; stable has a good batch of 2yos and this one is respected on his debut.
runners.comment: Make Believe colt; 40,000euros yearling; stable has good batch of 2yos; respected on debut

runners.name: Mrs Mole
runners.spotlight: 28-1 but she offered plenty to work on when fourth of ten at Leicester (7f, good) a fortnight ago; looked a touch raw on that first go and she's likely to improve with the experience under her belt; considered.
runners.comment: 28-1 but offered plenty to work on when fourth on debut a fortnight ago; considered

runners.name: Be More Ruthless
runners.spotlight: Rajasinghe filly; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Senor Carrots (5f AW; RPR 83) and Great Mates (8.6f AW; 81); dam 5f-1m winner (including 2yo; 94), sister to 6f 2yo winner Lady Katy, out of 5f/6f 2yo winner; yard has only sent out a handful of juveniles this term but is otherwise flying along of late; check the market.
runners.comment: Rajasinghe filly; yard 0-3 with this term's juveniles but otherwise flying along of late

runners.name: Platinum Force
runners.spotlight: By Blackbeard; 21,000euros foal, £35,000 yearling, 50,000euros breeze-up purchase in May; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Up The Anti (5.7f-1m; RPR 64) and Us Carmen (Polish 7f); dam unraced half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Invite, out of 1m2f winner/Oaks third Katiyra; one of the more likely debutants.
runners.comment: Sales price increased to 50,000euros as a breeze-up; one of the more likely debutants

runners.name: Parry Green
runners.spotlight: By Massaat; second foal; dam poor maiden (RPR 30), sister to French 1m4f winner Finjaans Rose, half-sister to winners Stepney (1m), Lady Charlie (7f 2yo) and Magical Flower (French 6.5f); already gelded and he doesn't feature among the leading newcomers on paper.
runners.comment: By Massaat; already gelded and he's not among the leading newcomers on paper

runners.name: Telegraphic
runners.spotlight: By Kameko; fifth foal; closely related to 7f winner Rousham (RPR 67), half-brother to winners First Of May (6f AW including 2yo; 85), Polka (5f AW; 72) and Zachary (5f/6f AW 2yo; 61); dam sprint maiden (55), out of five-time 1m Group 1 winner Soviet Song; interesting to see what the market makes of him on this debut.
runners.comment: By Kameko; from family of star Soviet Song; interesting to see how he goes in the market

runners.name: The First Drop
runners.spotlight: By Lope Y Fernandez; 35,000gns foal, 30,000gns yearling; first foal; dam twice-raced half-sister to useful winners Kings Gift (7f-1m2f including 2yo) and Queens Gift (5f/6f), out of 1m3f winner who stayed 2m; betting likely to provide some good clues on this introduction.
runners.comment: By Lope Y Fernandez; 35,000gns foal, 30,000gns yearling; betting a good guide on his debut

runners.name: Wells Fargo Stage
runners.spotlight: 50-1 when down the field over 6f at Redcar on last month's introduction; has plenty of winners in her pedigree but only of interest with market support.
runners.comment: 50-1 when down the field over 6f at Redcar on last month's introduction

runners.name: King Of Persia
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Amestris (5f 2yo; RPR 88) and Thisonesforyou (1m 2yo; 64); 25-1 when beaten just over 6l on his introduction here (6f, good) last Monday; worth a glance in the market but likely one for further down the line.
runners.comment: 25-1 and beaten just over 6l on debut here (6f, good) last Monday; worth a market check

runners.name: Geo
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when 12-1 and prevailing by a nose at Ayr (1m, good to firm) last month; beaten just a neck off this mark at Hamilton last Wednesday and she's likely to play a part again with Tom Marquand taking the reins; due to go up 2lb.
runners.comment: Won by a nose (1m, good to firm) last month; beaten a neck off this mark last Wednesday

runners.name: Harswell Ruby
runners.spotlight: Doubled her tally when winning at Thirsk (1m) in April; has continued to run with credit and it was a very solid fourth of 16 at York (1m1f, also good) 11 days ago; firmly in the picture again.
runners.comment: Very solid fourth of 16 at York (1m1f, also good) 11 days ago; firmly in the picture again

runners.name: Barley
runners.spotlight: Four runs here have yielded two seconds and a win in this race in 2024; down the field in last year's renewal but after some modest efforts he's rediscovered his level of late, going close at Hamilton last month and finishing a close third at Ripon three weeks ago; yard going well and he's in the mix again.
runners.comment: Four runs here have yielded two seconds and a win in this race in 2024; respected

runners.name: Marry The Night
runners.spotlight: Sole turf success came at Redcar (1m, good to firm) in August; he is now 16lb higher after a productive spell on AW and finished 12l adrift when returned to turf over C&D last month; down the list despite his yard being in fine form.
runners.comment: Now 16lb higher than sole turf win after good AW spell; 12l adrift over C&D last month

runners.name: Pepsea
runners.spotlight: Already gelded when promising second at Thirsk (good) on his debut in April and he was back at that level when fourth of 12 here (1m1f, good to soft) a fortnight ago; off-putting that his only run on good to firm was the worst of his first three starts and he again hung for pressure when fourth in a maiden at Beverley yesterday; makes handicap debut.
runners.comment: Has shown promise but also a tendency to hang, as at Beverley yesterday; handicap debut

runners.name: I Can Dance
runners.spotlight: Showed a consistent level when placed on all three starts (7f/1m) on a variety of surfaces last year; enters handicaps on his reappearance having been gelded and this opening mark is probably fair.
runners.comment: Placed all 3 starts (7f/1m) on variety of surfaces last year; gelded ahead of h'cap debut

runners.name: Popeye Doyle
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins for Hugo Palmer last season (1m, 8.5f); finished last on first two starts for new yard but a switch to blinkers prompted shock return to form when 80-1 and second of seven at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last Monday; interesting to see if the aids work a second time.
runners.comment: Shock return to form when second (1m, good to firm) in new blinkers last Monday

runners.name: Wetsand
runners.spotlight: Hit the ground running as a juvenile last term but her form tailed off at the backend and she hasn't rediscovered her best in four starts this year; did show a bit more on her last two starts, though, and her mark continues to come down.
runners.comment: Form tailed off at the backend last term; yet to rediscover best in four starts this year

runners.name: Runswick
runners.spotlight: Won a tidy six-runner Epsom novice (7f) in September and he built on an encouraging comeback/handicap debut third (also good to soft) last month when going very close at Chester (7.5f, soft) 12 days ago; quicker ground raises a question but he's going the right way and must be factored in.
runners.comment: Went very close (7.5f, soft) 12 days ago; quicker ground a query but he's still a player

runners.name: Aqua Bear
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish runner who landed a Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) on his November stable debut; subsequently gelded and it was a very creditable fourth in a warm event at Ascot on his handicap debut last month; weakened to finish down the field in another competitive race next time but this represents a drop in grade and he shaped like this stiffer test would suit at Ascot.
runners.comment: Very creditable fourth on handicap debut; blip next time came in a hot race; interesting

runners.name: Pension Pot
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who is yet to score but has shown ability across his six starts; latest third of 11 at Chester (7.5f, soft; acts on quicker) was a respectable effort and it's impossible to say he's not capable of better.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who is yet to score but has shown ability in six starts; not ruled out

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Albaydaa
runners.spotlight: Progressive since handicapping, placed twice in a row (6f/7f, good) before narrow win over this C&D (good to firm); others have stronger form but this well-bred sort could have plenty more left in the tank for her top yard.
runners.comment: Narrow C&D handicap win last time; could have plenty more left in the tank for top yard

runners.name: Ellusive Butterfly
runners.spotlight: She would have gone much closer with a clear run in a handicap at York (7f, good) last month and a return to front-running tactics bore fruit with a Listed win at Epsom (7f, good to soft) latest; below par on sole previous run on fast ground but she has leading form claims under a penalty.
runners.comment: Listed win at Epsom; leading claims under a penalty provided she's okay on the fast ground

runners.name: Golden Palace
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in Group/Listed races since winning last July on first two starts but she kept on well for a Listed third at Musselburgh (7f, good) last time and today's stiffer track could be right up her street; one to consider.
runners.comment: Kept on for Listed third at Musselburgh; this stiffer track could be right up her street

runners.name: Classic Cuvee
runners.spotlight: Strong form when winning Doncaster maiden (7f, good) last September on second start; disappointing since, including in Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, good) on last month's reappearance, and has something to prove, but she's not written off back down in trip in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Promising maiden winner last September; disappointing since but not written off

runners.name: Applesandpears
runners.spotlight: 2-3 in 2yo campaign and returned to form with close third to Albaydaa in C&D handicap (good to firm) last month; they reoppose on the same terms today and this lightly raced contender is open to further improvement, but she has something to find in today's company.
runners.comment: Close third in C&D handicap last month but has something to find in today's company

runners.name: Figjam
runners.spotlight: Listed runner-up last October (6f, heavy) and kept on for fourth of 15 in Listed race at Haydock (6f, good to soft) last month, providing optimism that she would be suited by 7f; however, she hasn't seen out 7f either side of that Haydock run, most recently sixth of seven at Musselburgh (good).
runners.comment: Kept on well for Listed fourth over 6f last month; hasn't seen out 7f either side of that

runners.name: Assaranca
runners.spotlight: She's had plenty of racing but has shown improvement on her last two runs, easily winning Class 4 handicap at Lingfield (7f, good to firm) before fifth of sixth to Ellusive Butterfly (who is now penalised) in Epsom Listed race (7f, good to soft), beaten about 4l; on the up and has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Listed 5th to Ellusive Butterfly at Epsom; that was a good run and she has each-way claims

runners.name: Confide In Me
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Southwell (7f, AW) in April and close second in Redcar novice (6f, good to firm) later that month; she should have a bright future but considerable improvement is needed if she's to threaten today.
runners.comment: 1st then 2nd on her first two starts but considerable improvement needed to threaten today

runners.name: Just A Girl
runners.spotlight: She's raced nine times and produced her best RPR yet when close second in C&D handicap (good to firm) last month, splitting Albaydaa and Applesandpears who were carrying 2lb more; probably vulnerable back up in grade.
runners.comment: Close second in C&D handicap last month but probably vulnerable back up in grade

runners.name: Stimulative Trip
runners.spotlight: Won at Newbury (7f, good to firm) last June on sole 2yo start and down the field in the Group 3 Fred Darling there (7f, good) on reappearance; beaten only about 3l when sixth of eight in Listed race at Chantilly (1m, good to soft) last month and shaped as though the return to 7f would help; her stable is in excellent form and she might have an each-way shout.
runners.comment: Drops back in trip with her stable in excellent form and she might have an each-way shout

races.race_name: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap

runners.name: Financer
runners.spotlight: All three wins came in 2024 over 1m2f (good/good to firm) but he was runner-up three times last season; fair effort at York last time and now has first-time blinkers replacing cheekpieces now trying 1m for the first time since his debut.
runners.comment: Fair effort at York last time but this is his first try over 1m since his debut in 2023
runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor

runners.name: Leadenhall
runners.spotlight: Creditable sixth of 16 at York (1m1f, good) before winning at Nottingham (8.5f, good) under Jake Dickson four days ago, making it two wins from his last three starts; escapes a penalty for that success and can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Made it two wins from his last three when winning under Jake Dickson four days ago; claims

runners.name: Rajapour
runners.spotlight: Sole win since joining this yard came in a first-time visor at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in December and he's ran some good races since the cheekpieces went on; didn't get the best of passages at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) nine days ago and can be treated as if still in form; up in grade but could go well.
runners.comment: Has ran some good races since cheekpieces went on; didn't get the best of runs last time

runners.name: Priapos
runners.spotlight: Added to his two AW successes since joining these connections when winning over this C&D 25 days ago (good to firm); best form has been on quick ground and he's a contender off this 3lb higher mark; cheekpieces, worn once previously, now return.
runners.comment: Up 3lb for his win over this C&D 25 days ago; cheekpieces return; rates a contender

runners.name: Naples
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground maiden winner at Listowel in September when trained by Paddy Twomey; well beaten in the Spring Mile on his yard debut but his two efforts since (1m2f) have been a bit better; cheekpieces now go on but he wouldn't want the ground too lively.
runners.comment: Heavy-ground maiden winner at Listowel; cheekpieces go on but quick ground a concern

runners.name: Swift Salian
runners.spotlight: Can front-run and acts on most ground; most recent of his three wins came here on soft ground over 1m1f last June and he's been in fine form this year, finishing runner-up in each of his three starts; once again has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Won here on soft over 1m1f last June; runner-up in each of his three starts this year

runners.name: Be Frank
runners.spotlight: Won at Newmarket (7f, good to form) on his reappearance last year off a 2lb lower mark than today's and was third of 15 over this trip next time; made some late gains from off the pace on his return at Chester (7f, good to soft) 12 days ago, shaping as if this longer trip was worth another go.
runners.comment: Made some late gains from off the pace at Chester last time and steps back up in trip

runners.name: Altareq
runners.spotlight: Promise in three starts for the Gosden stable and he caught the eye when third of 15 at York (1m2f, good) on last month's stable debut; again shaped promisingly at Hamilton (8.5f, good) next time, despite the steady gallop not suiting, but was disappointing back at York 12 days ago, albeit he may have not been helped by ending up towards the far rail; drops in grade and he's the type to bounce back if getting this run to suit.
runners.comment: Disappointing last time but caught the eye on his previous two starts for this stable

runners.name: Canvas
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer (can front run) who won three times last year, twice at Doncaster (1m) and at York (1m, good); reappeared from 203 days off with a solid effort at Newmarket (1m, good) last month but he was withdrawn after becoming upset in the stalls at Doncaster on his last intended start.
runners.comment: Won three times last year and posted a solid effort on his reappearance at Newmarket

runners.name: Lexington Jet
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has won four times since blinkers were added (7f/1m), most recently over 7f at Chester last month off 3lb lower; didn't get home there over 1m2f (good to soft) 11 days ago but should be happier back at this trip.
runners.comment: Front-runner; won four times since blinkers went on (7f/1m); didn't stay 1m2f last time

runners.name: Vixey
runners.spotlight: Won four times last year (7f/7.5f) and also posted some other good efforts, including when third of 21 in a competitive handicap at York (7f, good) in October; didn't settle well enough at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in November when last seen but the break may have done her some good.
runners.comment: Won four times last year (7f/7.5f); didn't settle well enough at Chelmsford when last seen

runners.name: Mr Lincoln
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 22 starts came at Cork in April 2023; fourth over hurdles at Perth before finishing second of ten behind an improving filly at Ayr (1m2f, soft) five days ago; drops back in trip with place claims.
runners.comment: 1-22; bumped into an improving filly at Ayr over 1m2f last time and has place claims

runners.name: Degale
runners.spotlight: Often front-runner who can race freely; sole win from ten starts came in a Ripon novice (1m, good) in August 2024 but his best effort since undergoing wind surgery came when a 1l third of 11 at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last month; below his best in two starts since.
runners.comment: 1-10; third of 11 at Nottingham last month but below his best in two starts since

runners.name: Enola Grey
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer whose record on turf reads 1-17 compared to 6-43 on the AW; shaped as if still in form when sixth of 11 at Lingfield (7f, AW) 117 days ago but may need her first outing since.
runners.comment: 1-17 on turf compared to 6-43 on the AW; off since finishing sixth on the AW in February

runners.name: Persuasion
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-3 at Musselburgh when winning there over 7f in April off a 1lb lower mark than today's; beaten only 4l when seventh of 19 at York (7f, good) 11 days ago but stamina has to be a concern on just his third try over a 1m.
runners.comment: All six wins have been over 7f; stamina has to be a concern on just his third try over 1m

runners.name: Glenfinnan
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden over 1m at two but all four wins since have been over shorter, the most recent at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm) last month; too free at York (7f) 11 days ago and he'll need to settle better now returned to this trip with the hood back on (worn once in 2023).
runners.comment: Won over 7.5f at Beverley last month but too free at York last time; hood goes back on

runners.name: Billyb
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, the most recent coming on the AW at Southwell off this mark in November; runner-up off a 4lb lower mark in this race in 2024 but he hasn't threatened in three starts this year; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Runner-up in this in 2024 off 4lb lower but hasn't threatened in three starts this year

races.race_name: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Wine Dark Sea
runners.spotlight: Fairly unexposed sort who showed his best form when taking 14-runner Ascot handicap (1m4f, good to firm) seven weeks ago; that race has worked out well, with the third and fourth winning big handicaps at Royal Ascot and at Epsom on Derby day respectively, so a 5lb rise now looks on the lenient side; form of his close second on final start of last year was also boosted at last week's royal meeting and he's a key contender.
runners.comment: Form of his last two starts could barely be working out better and he's a key contender

runners.name: Gaelic Approach
runners.spotlight: He has been doing well since being gelded and tackling handicaps on a sound surface, winning four-runner event at Leicester (1m4f) and three-runner race at Redcar (1m6f); far from disgraced in a Class 2 at Doncaster last time, bringing this year's form figures to 21213; not fully exposed and likely capable of better yet.
runners.comment: Form figures of 21213 this year; not fully exposed and likely capable of better yet
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin

runners.name: Wanderlust
runners.spotlight: Ex-French soft-ground winner who had been gelded when 50-1 and a solid fourth at Chester (10.3f, good) on his stable debut last month; outpaced over 1m2f on a fast surface next time so this stiffer test should suit and Tom Marquand takes over.
runners.comment: Solid fourth on yard debut; outpaced latest; not ruled out up in trip with Marquand aboard

runners.name: Alpine Sierra
runners.spotlight: Seemingly exposed gelding who has suddenly got on a roll since going back up in trip, scoring on good ground at Hamilton (1m3f), Musselburgh (12.5f) and over C&D last month; has to defy a further 4lb rise in a better race from the widest stall but he can't be ignored attempting a four-timer.
runners.comment: 8yo who suddenly got on a roll going back up in trip; not ignored in hunt for four-timer

runners.name: Ludo's Landing
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner whose three appearances here have yielded two wins (7f and 1m) back in 2022/2023; also finished runner-up in this race (good to firm) in 2024 and he was back on track when fairly close third of four at Hamilton (1m5f, good to firm) four weeks ago; winner and second have won subsequently and he warrants consideration.
runners.comment: Good track record includes second spot in this race in 2024; solid 3rd latest; considered

runners.name: Dunkeld Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Came out on top in a tight finish for prestigious female amateur jockeys' race at York (1m4f, good) 11 days ago; that made it a fifth win for this progressive filly and she'll need considering after a 2lb nudge.
runners.comment: Progressive filly who just landed prestigious female amateur jockeys' race latest; player

runners.name: Secret Beach
runners.spotlight: Two 1m4f wins for Ben Brookhouse on a sound surface but he finished well adrift on his reappearance and first start since wind surgery at Nottingham in April; much more competitive when fifth of 15 at Chester (12.5f, good) one month later; was well drawn that day but it's still early days for this yard.
runners.comment: Well drawn when encouraging fifth of 15 last time; still early days for this yard

runners.name: Eroico
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in Irish maidens for Noel Meade but yet to get going in two starts for this yard, latterly in a hood (now removed) at Thirsk last month; this is just her third handicap but it's a tough one in which to stage a revival.
runners.comment: Showed ability in Irish maidens for Noel Meade but yet to get going for this yard

runners.name: Austrian Theory
runners.spotlight: Not the force of old but, after a couple of modest efforts, his latest fifth of 18 at York (10.5f, good; first-time cheekpieces) was a step back in the right direction; shaped as though this stiffer test might suit on that occasion so interesting to see if the headgear works a second time for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Latest fifth (first-time cheekpieces) was a step back in the right direction; yard in form

runners.name: Golden West
runners.spotlight: 2-2 as a juvenile but he didn't progress at three (quite highly tried) and left Karl Burke for 25,000gns that October; missed last year but there was plenty of encouragement when fourth at Chester (10.5f, good to soft) 11 days ago; fairly quick second run back from a long absence sounds a note of caution but his yard's in fine form; respected.
runners.comment: Promising fourth on yard debut 11 days ago after big absence; quick return but respected

runners.name: Inappropriate
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner for Harry Eustace (1m2f, good) who also scored twice over hurdles for Jedd O'Keeffe; became very inconsistent for latter yard, however, and he was picked up for £20,000 one month ago; refitted tongue-tie and first-time cheekpieces for this stable debut.
runners.comment: Became inconsistent and sold for £20,000 one month ago; new headgear combination

runners.name: Trojan Sun
runners.spotlight: Made it win number six when scoring here (1m6f, good; has won at up to 2m2f) last Monday; 5lb penalty makes life tough and he's never won at shorter than 1m6f; evidently in good heart, though.
runners.comment: Win number six when scoring here (1m6f; never won at shorter) 9 days ago; 5lb penalty

runners.name: Nanoscience
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on her AW reappearance in April and following a solid handicap debut she struck at Wetherby (also 7f, good to firm; made all) three weeks ago; received only a 1lb nudge for that neck success and there's a good chance we've yet to see the best of her.
runners.comment: Progressive filly who received only a 1lb nudge after latest win; must be considered

runners.name: Shushu
runners.spotlight: Both starts at 7f; encouraging debut when second of five at Haydock (soft) in May and she was away briskly when making all at Yarmouth (good to soft) a fortnight ago; that was a ready win and this better ground ought not to be a concern on pedigree; key contender on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Ready win on second start; better ground no issue on pedigree; key player on h'cap debut

runners.name: Donna Nook
runners.spotlight: Took 17 goes to strike for this yard but it all came together at Ripon (1m, good) three weeks ago; will need extra up 3lb but that was the first time she sported this headgear combination and today's stiff 7f is probably not a problem.
runners.comment: Wore this headgear combo when winning (1m, good) three weeks ago; this trip probably fine

runners.name: Who Wants Me
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden who went very close over C&D just over a year ago; finished down the field in this race (also soft) nine days later but was back in some form from August following wind surgery; reappearance sixth in a blanket finish at Hamilton last month was a solid effort but slow starts have become a theme and she's 2lb 'wrong'.
runners.comment: 15-race maiden who went close over C&D a year ago but 2lb 'wrong' and others appeal more

runners.name: Rogue Attraction
runners.spotlight: Went fairly close on her debut over C&D in August and she made all for an easy Catterick maiden (6f, also good) win three weeks later; came up short in Group 3 and Listed company subsequently but cheekpieces go on and she's impossible to rule out ahead of this reappearance/handicap debut; watch the market.
runners.comment: Easy mdn win; cheekpieces on & can't be ruled out on return/h'cap debut; watch the market

runners.name: Mostar Dreams
runners.spotlight: Mostly a hold-up mare; two wins from just five starts here feature a success over this trip (soft) two years ago and another over 6f (good to firm) last May; on her third trainer when scoring over 1m at Newcastle (AW) in January but she'll need to be better than ever to defy this mark on her first start since February; this could set up quite nicely, though.
runners.comment: Won twice here; needs to be better than ever from this mark but it could set up nicely
runners.owner: J Fyffe, A Edmondson & L McGurty

races.race_name: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Madam Secretary
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly was not particularly strong in the market when a 5l third of eight for her debut at Listowel (6.5f, good); dam is by Galileo, so some reservations about the suitability of the trip; her brother Amadeus Mozart was second in last week's Golden Gate Handicap (1m2f); still, could pose a lively threat.
runners.comment: Light in the market first time out at Listowel, should pose a more lively threat this time
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Hip Hop Chrissy
runners.spotlight: Second in a barrier trial at this venue last month; made a satisfactory debut five days later when third at Listowel; this opposition looks more demanding.
runners.comment: Satisfactory debut when third at Listowel; this opposition looks more demanding
runners.owner: Eclipse T'bred Partners/Gaynor Bloodstock LLC/F Gallagher

runners.name: Lady Patrona
runners.spotlight: Second in a seven-runner maiden over C&D on debut; enough merit in her Curragh fourth (behind the Albany Stakes winner Sun Goddess) to suggest she can go close.
runners.comment: Second over C&D on debut, enough merit in her Curragh fourth to suggest she can go close
runners.owner: Miss Kate Harrington & Mrs John Harrington & B Phelan

runners.name: Celestial Crown
runners.spotlight: Just touched off in an early-season 5f maiden at Cork; winner Velozee won her next race and has confirmed a fair standard of form in Pattern races won by Victorious, fourth at this venue and eighth in last week's Queen Mary; a leading contender.
runners.comment: Just touched off in an early-season 5f maiden at Cork, form boosted by the winner Velozee

runners.name: Shi Yang
runners.spotlight: 55,000gns Y (n/s 75,000gns 2yo); first foal; unraced dam closely related to useful winner First Winter, half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Aesop's Fables (Prix Jean Prat), out of useful winning close relative of Derby/Arc winner Lammtarra.
runners.comment: Unraced dam is a half-sister to Prix Jean Prat winner Aesop's Fables, in good hands

runners.name: April Mist
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister by Cotai Glory to a 6f/7f winner Kisskodi; dam 7f AW winner, half-sister to 6f Listed winner Castle Hill Cassie.
runners.comment: Half-sister by Cotai Glory to a 6f/7f winner, dam 7f AW winner, yard's second string

runners.name: Bettercalltheboss
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; Nando Parrado filly is a sister to winners in Spain and Italy; dam placed 1m2f 2yo, half-sister to Group 3 winners Abjer and Gloomy Sunday; fair pedigree but not a likely debut winner in a maiden of this standard.
runners.comment: Fair pedigree but not a likely debut winner in a maiden of this standard

runners.name: Brazilian Diva
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in third on 5f Dundalk debut; out of her depth in 6f Curragh Group 3 last month; still looked a bit raw when fifth of 17 in a recent 6f maiden at Fairyhouse, but that was an encouraging display nonetheless.
runners.comment: Showed some promise at Fairyhouse, more likely one for nurseries in due course

runners.name: Red Primrose
runners.spotlight: Failed to sell at 220,000gns at Newmarket last October; fifth foal; half-sister by Blackbeard to winners Awaken (Group-placed 6f 2yo and Alrahy (KSA 1m); dam 1m2f AW winner, half-sister to multiple 1m2f Group 1 winner Twice Over; probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Failed to sell at 220,000gns at Newmarket last October, probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Tatianna
runners.spotlight: Made an encouraging debut over 5f at Cork last month; failed to build on that in a 6f Fairyhouse race nine days later.
runners.comment: Made an encouraging debut over 5f at Cork, failed to build on that in a 6f Fairyhouse race

runners.name: Mini Cotai
runners.spotlight: Best efforts this year have come over this trip on soft ground, including at Down Royal last time from 7lb out of the handicap; only 2lb 'wrong' today but the drying ground might not suit as well.
runners.comment: 2nd at Down Royal last time from 7lb out of the h'cap; only 2lb 'wrong' today; best on sft

runners.name: Jazzit
runners.spotlight: Yet to win but his three best runs have come on AW; not bad runs at Navan over 6f-5f last twice but needs more down 2lb.
runners.comment: Maiden; not bad runs at Navan over 6f-5f last twice but needs more down 2lb

runners.name: Steel Magnolia
runners.spotlight: Third runner for yard; best form has come over this trip on soft ground, winning at Bellewstown last autumn; fine efforts back there and at Cork this season; drying ground isn't in her favour.
runners.comment: Fine efforts at Bellewstown and Cork this season; drying ground isn't in her favour

runners.name: Happy Henry
runners.spotlight: Finally got off the mark at Navan over this trip last time with cheekpieces replacing other headgear; was a cosy winner then and should go well even off 8lb higher with a visor back on.
runners.comment: Cosy winner last time and should go well even off 8lb higher with a visor back on

runners.name: Hooves Your Daddy
runners.spotlight: Good runs in defeat over this trip at Dundalk and Down Royal lately but was below that level over 6f at Fairyhouse; respected back to 5f.
runners.comment: Was below her best over 6f at Fairyhouse last time; respected back to 5f

runners.name: Sayonara
runners.spotlight: Some decent runs in defeat on AW over 5f-6f during the winter but was poor at Fairyhouse on recent return; has to improve hugely on that.
runners.comment: Was poor at Fairyhouse on recent return; has to improve hugely on that

runners.name: Platino Bianco
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on AW at Newcastle over 6f last winter; hit and miss since and has to rebound from a poor Fairyhouse effort.
runners.comment: Hit and miss lately and has to rebound from a poor Fairyhouse effort

runners.name: Another Nice'day
runners.spotlight: Best effort came over this trip at Down Royal last year; poor over 7f and 6f both runs this year; has to do much better but coming back to 5f might help.
runners.comment: Poor over 7f and 6f both runs this year; has to do much better but back to 5f might help

runners.name: You Smiled
runners.spotlight: The best of three maiden runs came at Cork last time but was still beaten 9l; lacks experience for a race of this nature.
runners.comment: The best of three maiden runs came at Cork last time but still beaten 9l; lacks experience

runners.name: Snowballs
runners.spotlight: Maiden anticipated the start at Cork last time and was always in rear; can do better but looks the yard's second-string behind Happy Henry.
runners.comment: Anticipated the start at Cork last time; can do better but looks the yard's second-string

runners.name: Whatswrongnow
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been a close runner-up in three of his last four starts between 5f-6f; an extra 3lb for his Fairyhouse second doesn't help but should be involved again.
runners.comment: A 3lb hike for his Fairyhouse second doesn't help but should be involved again

runners.name: Whats It All About
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in handicaps at Dundalk and the Curragh lately so has to find improvement back to 5f.
runners.comment: Well beaten in handicaps at Dundalk and the Curragh lately so has to find improvement

runners.name: Spiced Gold
runners.spotlight: Not a bad effort at Down Royal over 5f but wasn't as good over 6f at Fairyhouse since; dropped 3lb so mightn't be far away back to this trip.
runners.comment: Decent effort at Down Royal over 5f; not as good over 6f since; mightn't be far away

runners.name: Bel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Well beaten lately and officially rated only 31 so not easy to fancy.
runners.comment: Well beaten lately and officially rated only 31 so not easy to fancy
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Polar Bear
runners.spotlight: Thed last of his two wins came at Bellewstown in 2023; some decent runs in defeat this year but was poor at Fairyhouse last time and has to rebound.
runners.comment: Some decent runs in defeat this year; was poor at Fairyhouse last time and has to rebound

runners.name: Hero Of The Hour
runners.spotlight: Seven wins (incl' at this track) between turf and AW over 5f-6f for former yard; not a bad stable debut at Fairyhouse last time and can be in the mix with some improvement.
runners.comment: Not a bad stable debut at Fairyhouse last time and can be in the mix with some improvement

runners.name: Mehmar
runners.spotlight: Generally regressive in the last while and was poor in a Fairyhouse claimer last time in a first-time visor; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Poor in a Fairyhouse claimer last time in a first-time visor; others make more appeal

runners.name: Ballysax Lil' Mick
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Tipperary last August over this trip; some solid efforts since including on comeback behind Happy Henry at Navan; can improve from that and is in the mix.
runners.comment: Decent comeback behind Happy Henry at Navan; can improve from that and is in the mix

runners.name: Realtin Fantasy
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-53 with both coming over this trip; was hit and miss last year without scoring; needs to be ready on return.
runners.comment: Was hit and miss last year without scoring; needs to be ready on return

runners.name: Apache Star
runners.spotlight: All three wins came when trained in UK; has been below form for this yard and has to stage a revival even from a dropping mark.
runners.comment: Below form for this yard and has to stage a revival even from a dropping mark

runners.name: Billboa
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins but hasn't been at his best since returning from an absence last autumn; his mark is dropping and a tongue-tie is added but others preferred.
runners.comment: Out of form on AW; his mark is dropping and a tongue-tie is added but others preferred

runners.name: Tawaazon
runners.spotlight: Thed last of his nine wins came over 6f at this track last year; has been disappointing in two starts this year but is now 7lb below that last winning mark; needs a return to form.
runners.comment: Is now 7lb below his last winning mark but needs a return to form

runners.name: Livingston Range
runners.spotlight: Similar efforts in defeat both starts this year; travelled well at Fairyhouse last time over 6f but didn't find a lot off the bridle; coming back to this trip might help.
runners.comment: Travelled well last time over 6f but then didn't find a lot; this trip might help

runners.name: Reinforce
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over this trip in 2024; lost his form soon afterwards and has been absent almost a year so is best watched unless market support comes.
runners.comment: Has been absent almost a year so is best watched unless market support comes

races.race_name: Race & Stay Irish Racing Tours Rated Race

runners.name: Go Just Do It
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in three races at the Curragh last season, all won by Ballydoyle-trained colts; most meaningful form when third of five in a 7f Group 2 won by Constitution River; odds-on for a comfortable 7f maiden win at Gowran on reappearance; failed to stay, and outclassed in any case in the Irish 2,000 Guineas; best of these, but hard to be sure that he has the requisite speed.
runners.comment: 7f winner, outclassed in Irish 2,000 Guineas, may get away with a trip shorter than ideal

runners.name: Amiata
runners.spotlight: 5f Navan maiden winner during an eight-race juvenile campaign; useful form in sprint handicaps this term, winning two of his last three starts; stuck to his task gamely in a 5f premier handicap at the Curragh three weeks ago; chance improved by rider's claim.
runners.comment: Solid form in winning two sprint handicaps this term, chance improved by rider's claim

runners.name: Gavoo
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season, including a 6f Dundalk event in which today's rival Amiata was the unplaced favourite; kept on for fourth place in a C&D handicap on seasonal debut; back to 6f here after a nondescript run when favourite for a 7f handicap at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Won twice at two, had a below-par Amiata behind at Dundalk, 2026 form is less persuasive

runners.name: Oro Blanco
runners.spotlight: Stable second-string when landing a 7f maiden at Fairyhouse on his third start; may continue on the upgrade but this is a tough task against three more experienced rivals.
runners.comment: Fairyhouse 7f winner, this is a tough task against three more experienced rivals

races.race_name: Al Shira'aa Racing Irish EBF Jannah Rose Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)

runners.name: Cameo
runners.spotlight: Curragh 7f maiden winner at two (soft to heavy); took a big step forward with a convincing win in the Listed Oaks Trial at Lingfield (good to firm) last month on her sixth start, and her first run beyond 1m; appeared to have a good chance of staying the Oaks trip, but faded from over two furlongs down; will be hard to beat if the race has not left a mark.
runners.comment: Oaks Trial winner at Lingfield, Epsom effort was quite tame but should make amends now
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: La Fogata
runners.spotlight: Useful form over 1m as a juvenile; beaten 5l in a six-runner Curragh Group 3, won a Punchestown maiden; heavy ground a possible excuse for a poor run on final start for Ger Lyons; finished in front of well-fancied stablemate Eretria when second to Caught U Sleeping (levels) over C&D on first outing for this team; another solid run at Navan last time.
runners.comment: Second to Caught U Sleeping over C&D, 3lb better off, another solid run at Navan last time

runners.name: Della Pace
runners.spotlight: Kempton maiden winner at two; beaten in a four-runner handicap at Wolverhampton; had shown better form in making the frame in two Listed races over 1m2f on turf, both at Goodwood; hard to see her winning at this level.
runners.comment: AW winner, decent form in 1m2f Listed races on turf, hard to see her winning at this level

runners.name: Sinmara
runners.spotlight: Gowran maiden winner; creditable fourth in the 1m Group 3 Athasi Stakes at the Curragh, with Caught U Sleeping and Pollenca among those behind; out of her depth in the Irish 1,000 Guineas; useful form, but ominous that the champion jockey deserts her to ride the once-raced Rebel Moon
runners.comment: Useful but ominous that the champion jockey deserts her to ride the once-raced Rebel Moon

runners.name: Caught U Sleeping
runners.spotlight: Winner and Listed-placed over 7f at two; hooded when disappointing in Guineas trial over that trip on comeback; made some late progress in a 1m Group 3 at the Curragh; much-improved only five days later when beating La Fogata in a Listed contest over C&D.
runners.comment: Much-improved when beating La Fogata in a C&D Listed race, not much between the pair

runners.name: Pollenca
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Curragh maiden on heavy in March; ran green and was blocked in her run when starting to stay on from rear in the last quarter-mile of a 1m Group 3 event at the same venue early last month; reappeared in a C&D Listed race five days later, kept on in fourth, needs to reverse form with Caught U Sleeping and La Fogata to win here.
runners.comment: 92-rated filly, needs to reverse form with Caught U Sleeping and La Fogata to win here

runners.name: Rebel Moon
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega filly cost 500,000gns as a yearling; made a promising debut when winning a maiden over the extended 1m1f at Gowran in March; especially good effort considering she was the stable's second-string; beaten 2 3/4l in third was her short-priced stablemate Johanna Walsh, who went close in last week's Ribblesdale; promising filly.
runners.comment: Relegated her high-class stablemate Johanna Walsh to third when making a winning debut

runners.name: Ohmali
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m at Gowran last year on heavy ground but handles better too; stays this trip but has been hit and miss this year so needs his best.
runners.comment: Won over 1m at Gowran last year; stays this trip but has been hit and miss this year

runners.name: Neon Eyes
runners.spotlight: Built upon her Gowran handicap debut over 1m when just denied by Amrum over 1m1.5f at the same track last time; 2lb better off with that rival today so is a player.
runners.comment: Just denied by Amrum over 1m1.5f at Gowran last time; 2lb better off so a player

runners.name: Elusive Duke
runners.spotlight: Leopardstown winner last year off 58 for former yard was just denied at Gowran last time, his best effort since; should go well off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Leopardstown winner last year was just denied at Gowran last time; go well off 1lb higher

runners.name: Casheldale Lass
runners.spotlight: The second of her two wins last year came over C&D; was just denied at Leopardstown but has to rebound from a lesser Fairyhouse run.
runners.comment: C&D winner was just denied at Leopardstown but has to rebound from a lesser Fairyhouse run

runners.name: Killyclogher Rose
runners.spotlight: Decent efforts in defeat on AW last twice between 1m2f-1m4f; has a chance on handicap debut after a break if as effective on turf.
runners.comment: Decent efforts in defeat on AW last twice between 1m2f-1m4f; chance on h'cap debut

runners.name: Glendower
runners.spotlight: Maiden hasn't had many runs for his age, five of them over hurdles; not bad efforts lately after an absence in a Ballinrobe maiden and Limerick claimer; can be a player.
runners.comment: Maiden hasn't had many runs and not bad efforts lately after an absence; a player

runners.name: Oust
runners.spotlight: Put in decent efforts on both handicap starts over similar trips to this, at Roscommon and Down Royal; just bumped into a well-handicapped rival last time but can be a player again.
runners.comment: Decent efforts both h'cap starts over similar trips to this; can be a player again

runners.name: Anchors Away
runners.spotlight: Maiden has posted some decent efforts in defeat including at Fairyhouse last time over 1m2f; can be involved with the tongue-tie added then retained.
runners.comment: Some decent efforts in defeat incl' at Fairyhouse last time; can be involved

runners.name: Rising Sky
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip on AW in March wearing first-time blinkers but has been poor twice since so has questions to answer now.
runners.comment: Won over this trip on AW but poor twice since so questions to answer

runners.name: Jazz Forever
runners.spotlight: First reserve; AW winner in 2024 is a maiden on turf; just denied at Navan last time so should go well if building on that.
runners.comment: AW winner in 2024; just denied at Navan last time so go well if building on that; reserve

runners.name: Dancing Steve
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb lower at Navan last year but lost his form badly at the end of last season; needs a return to his best after time away.
runners.comment: Won off 2lb lower at Navan last year but lost form at the end of last season; needs best

runners.name: Amrum
runners.spotlight: Narrowly got off the mark on handicap debut at Gowran last time, beating today's rival Neon Eyes a head over 1m1.5f; cheekpieces added and raised 7lb but should stay this trip so respected.
runners.comment: Narrowly off the mark on h'cap debut at Gowran last time; cheekpieces added and raised 7lb

runners.name: Doctor Grace
runners.spotlight: The latest of her five wins came over 1m4f at Roscommon in May; unseated early next time and 1m1.5f looked too short last time at Gowran; has to rebound.
runners.comment: Latest of five wins came over 1m4f at Roscommon; 1m1.5f looked too short last time

runners.name: Cameclose
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; not bad runs at Dundalk/Killarney over this trip but then beaten 10l at Cork; rebounded with a second in a  Ballinrobe claimer on Monday; needs more from 7lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Not bad runs at Dundalk/Killarney over this trip but beaten 10l at Cork last time; reserve

runners.name: Derry Lad
runners.spotlight: The last of his four wins came three years ago; not a bad fifth at Leopardstown last time in a higher grade than this; needs more but shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: The last of four wins came three years ago; not a bad 5th at Leopardstown; needs more

runners.name: Forlio
runners.spotlight: Won by 5l at Navan last autumn and basically struggled off higher marks since; been hurdling lately and needs more off this mark.
runners.comment: Won by 5l at Navan last autumn; hurdling lately and needs more off this mark

runners.name: Priorstown
runners.spotlight: Just glimpses of ability in three maidens and has to raise his game even dropping to handicaps; worth a market look, all the same.
runners.comment: Just glimpses of ability in maidens and has to raise his game even dropping to h'caps

runners.name: I'm Flattered
runners.spotlight: Was well beaten in three Flat maidens in 2024; over hurdles since, winning a Kilbeggan handicap last August; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three Flat maidens in 2024; hurdle winner since; others appeal more

runners.name: Krasimir
runners.spotlight: Has posted solid runs in defeat in five starts up to a mile; hung when under pressure at Leopardstown last time and blinkers are added today; needs to find a bit more to win but has to be considered.
runners.comment: Hung under pressure at Leopardstown last time and blinkers are added today; needs more

runners.name: Listentodwindblow
runners.spotlight: Took a rating of 95 into this season after finishing 6.75l behind Christmas Day in a Leopardstown Group 3 on final 2yo start; hasn't run to that level in both starts this season; still top-rated in this but vulnerable too; looks stable first-string.
runners.comment: Hasn't run to best in both runs this season; still top-rated in this but vulnerable too

runners.name: Kalir
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent in defeat in four maidens under varying ground conditions over similar trips to this; was a head behind Saliba at Gowran last time and will have to find improvement for being gelded since.
runners.comment: A head behind Saliba last time and will have to find improvement for being gelded since
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Dark Leader
runners.spotlight: Finished a close second on debut over 7f at Limerick but didn't build on that over a mile at Killarney since when raced keenly; has to resume progress but yard is in form after two Royal Ascot wins.
runners.comment: Has to resume progress but yard is in form after two Royal Ascot wins

runners.name: Transcript
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam 1m4f Oaks winner (RPR 117), sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Together Forever (dam of City Of Troy), half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Lord Shanakill, out of unraced half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 1 winner Spanish Fern; fantastic pedigree and needs a market check on debut; tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Fantastic pedigree of City Of Troy and needs a market check on debut; tongue-tie
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Pinoroyale
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; closely related to 7f-1m4f winner Golden Lyra (inc 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 109), half-brother to winners incl' Going Gone (1m2f/1m4f; 103); cost E75,000 as a yearling; dam unraced sister to German Derby winner Sea The Moon; lots to like on paper and one to consider if strong in the market.
runners.comment: Lots to like on paper and one to consider if strong in the market

runners.name: Nthea Spirit
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; dam unplaced 5f 2yo (RPR 55), half-sister to 6f 2yo Listed winner Hold Your Colour, out of 7f 2yo winner; by a dual-purpose sire; a E2,000 vendor buyback as a foal; best watched on debut for future reference.
runners.comment: A E2,000 vendor buyback as a foal; best watched on debut for future reference

runners.name: Cello Sonata
