runners.spotlight: Placed for first time in five-race career when keeping-on third over C&D last month; needs to build on that performance today but that's possible.
runners.comment: Placed for first time in five-race career when keeping-on third over C&D last month
runners.owner: Rotary Club Of Kwai Chung Syndicate

runners.name: Meowth
runners.spotlight: Consistent 5yo who kept on well for second over C&D three weeks ago and has now made the frame on seven of his nine starts since winning here in November; likely to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Consistent 5yo who kept on well for second over C&D three weeks ago

runners.name: Warriors Dream
runners.spotlight: Still 4lb higher than when winning at Sha Tin in March, but was placed there last month and arguably bettered than performance when fifth from stall 10 last time; well drawn here; one to consider.
runners.comment: Placed at Sha Tin last month and ran well again from wide stall a fortnight later
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Stephen Ho Wai Man, F Ho Ka Chun & D Ho Ka Chung

runners.name: World Hero
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this year; placed off today's mark here last month but wasn't at same level here a fortnight later and stall 11 asks a lot of him today.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this year; stall 11 asks a lot of him today

runners.name: Nebraskan
runners.spotlight: Reserve; revitalised 5yo who twice made all over C&D in April/May; managed only sixth off this mark three weeks ago, though, and will probably find others better handicapped again.
runners.comment: Twice made all over C&D in April/May but others in the field look better handicapped
runners.owner: Kenneth Lam Sze Ken, Timothy Junior Lam & T Fok Kwong Hang

runners.name: Tactical Horizons
runners.spotlight: Well down the field at triple-digit odds on all three Hong Kong starts (5f-6f); dropped in grade today but still can't be recommended.
runners.comment: Well down the field at triple-digit odds on all three Hong Kong starts
runners.owner: Elvis Kwok Kam Chuen, Winkle Lee Wing Ho Et Al

runners.name: Gameplayer Elite
runners.spotlight: Led close home over C&D in April and is now 2-5 since rejoining Douglas Whyte this year; well drawn again today and still well handicapped on older form; shortlisted.
runners.comment: 2-5 since rejoining current stable and still well handicapped on older form
runners.owner: Lam Yau Pik, Rex Lam Lam & Monica Lam Mung Ni

runners.name: Smart Fighter
runners.spotlight: Won from the front over C&D in April, but was well below that form on next two outings and makes only limited appeal from stall 10.
runners.comment: Twice well held since front-running win in April and has awkward draw today

runners.name: Fun Elite
runners.spotlight: Placed over 5f in February but recent 6f form has been disappointing and he faces a tough task from stall 11 today.
runners.comment: Recent 6f form has been disappointing and he faces a tough task from stall 11 today

runners.name: Free Pony
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over C&D since his breakthrough win here in December, but is not making any progress and has a tough draw today; others preferred.
runners.comment: Placed twice since C&D win in December but has tough draw today; others preferred
runners.owner: Edwin Fong Yick Chung, Edmund Ho Kwok Shing & So Wai Keung

runners.name: Joy Star
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six starts but did pretty well to finish sixth under a positive ride from stall 14 at Sha Tin last month and has a much better draw here.
runners.comment: 0-6 but did pretty well to finish sixth from stall 14 at Sha Tin last month
runners.owner: Lam King Wai & Dr & Mrs Lam Kai Cheong

runners.name: King Alloy
runners.spotlight: Consistent in Class 5 this season, but went up 7lb for last month's 5f win from stall 1 and has more on his plate in this higher grade.
runners.comment: Up 7lb for recent 5f course win; has more on his plate in this higher grade
runners.owner: Lun Ka Leung & Yeung Hiu Yu

runners.name: Majestic Delight
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual Class 5 winner this year, latterly when clinging on by a nose over C&D three weeks ago; looks vulnerable in this higher grade, though.
runners.comment: Held on by a nose over C&D this month; looks vulnerable in this higher grade

runners.name: Graceful Heart
runners.spotlight: Safely held on first two starts but belied 79-1 odds when fourth over C&D from stall 9 last month; much better drawn today; interesting to see whether he can build on that recent effort.
runners.comment: Improved form when fourth over C&D last month; much better draw today

runners.name: Looking Bright
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 0-9 in Hong Kong; appreciated drop to this grade when fifth at Sha Tin (5f) last month but this return to 6f is not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Ran okay over 5f when dropped to this grade last month; return to 6f not ideal

runners.name: Georgian Sigma
runners.spotlight: Close third over C&D three weeks ago and has now made the frame on all five appearances; ought to play another major role from stall 2.
runners.comment: In the frame all five starts and can play another major role from stall 2
runners.owner: Clement Cheng Ka Man, Alfred Choi & Roger Li Ka Chun

runners.name: Ryui Kokoroe
runners.spotlight: 0-9 in Hong Kong; suggested he might be building up to something when fourth over C&D in April but two lesser efforts have followed.
runners.comment: Fourth over C&D in April but two lesser efforts followed; now 0-9 in Hong Kong

runners.name: Cloud Nine
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; dropped out tamely after making the running over 5f at Sha Tin last month, but made the frame on four of his five 6f starts; back up in trip today; considered each-way.
runners.comment: Well beaten over 5f last month but in the frame on four of his five 6f starts

runners.name: Bits Superstar
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D on five of his nine starts this season; below form here last month but recent 2lb drop is helpful; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Below form here last month but has been placed five times over C&D this season
runners.owner: Tam Jin Rong & Zhao Xiao Na

runners.name: Danica's Choice
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in Hong Kong; kept on for respectable fifth over C&D on recent stable debut but double-digit draw makes things awkward today.
runners.comment: Respectable fifth over C&D on stable debut but wide draw makes things awkward

runners.name: Spirit Of Peace
runners.spotlight: Yet to win this season but scores high marks for his consistency over C&D this year (mainly in higher-grade company) and did well to finish fourth under Zac Purton here last month, having been hampered and denied a clear run; good chance.
runners.comment: Scores high marks for his consistency over C&D this year; likely contender again
runners.owner: Fong Chung Man, Vincent Fong & Lucia Fong So Ching

runners.name: Good Luck Happy
runners.spotlight: Disappointed when last in action in February, but was runner-up over C&D on two of his first three starts and remains lightly raced; contender if back on song after a break.
runners.comment: Off since poor run in February but was second over C&D on two of first three starts
runners.owner: Siu Chak Wai, Siu Wai Chung & Siu Wing Kin

races.race_name: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Parents' Love
runners.spotlight: Reserve; exploited reduced mark at Sha Tin (5f) in April but didn't run well there on latest outing; others have more persuasive profiles.
runners.comment: Won off a reduced mark in April but others have more persuasive profiles
runners.owner: Edmund Ho Kwok Shing, Joe Fong Fu Yuen Et Al

runners.name: Blue Illusion
runners.spotlight: Scored off today's mark over C&D in December but recent efforts have been disappointing; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: C&D winner off this mark in December, but recent efforts have been disappointing

runners.name: Metro Power
runners.spotlight: Just did enough to make all over 6f here in March; also suited by 5f and seemingly still in good form but needs to find something extra to overcome current mark.
runners.comment: 6f winner in March; also suited by 5f but has little margin for error off this mark

runners.name: Pegas
runners.spotlight: Won readily over 6f at Sha Tin in January, his fourth Hong Kong start, but has not built on that success since and is not sure to be suited by this drop back to 5f; favourable draw not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Yet to build upon 6f win in January and not sure to be suited by this drop back to 5f

runners.name: Together We Value
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in Hong Kong but has made good late headway over C&D on last three appearances and has good claims under Zac Purton if he gets a strong pace to aim at.
runners.comment: Made good late headway over C&D on last three outings; Purton takes the ride today
runners.owner: 25/26 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Sky Cap
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D in this higher grade since his two 6f Class 4 wins in March/April, but didn't fire when last in action and needs to bounce back with a career-best.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner this year; didn't fire last time; needs to rebound with career-best

runners.name: Copper Core
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; launched Hong Kong career with two low-key C&D runs and is seemingly in need of significant further help from the local handicapper.
runners.comment: Ex-Australian 4yo; launched Hong Kong career with two low-key C&D runs

runners.name: Robot Lucky Star
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who won quite comfortably over the straight 5f at Sha Tin in October and has made the frame on three of his four subsequent 6f starts; could still have a bigger run in him back at 5f but wide draw dents his appeal.
runners.comment: 5f winner; not yet fully exposed but wide draw dents his appeal today

runners.name: Horsepower
runners.spotlight: Close second three consecutive times over C&D since March; ought to give another good account but wide draw makes things awkward today and Zac Purton has deserted him.
runners.comment: Close second over C&D on last three outings but has a wide draw today
runners.owner: Michael T H Lee, Vanessa Lee & Amelia Lee

runners.name: Central Bank
runners.spotlight: Down the field on first two Hong Kong starts but last month's C&D fifth was much more encouraging and he's better drawn today; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Down the field on first two HK starts but last month's C&D fifth was better

runners.name: Straight To Glory
runners.spotlight: Reserve; three wins this year, the latest over the straight 5f at Sha Tin last month; held off this mark there since, though, and needs to kick on again.
runners.comment: Three wins this year (5f-6f); needs to kick on again to overcome this mark, though
runners.owner: Chan Tim Shing & Pang Wai Hak

runners.name: Red Elegance
runners.spotlight: Four-time C&D winner; well below best form this season and has stall 12 to contend with today.
runners.comment: Well below best form this season and has stall 12 to contend with today

runners.name: La Forza
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D on seasonal debut in September but both subsequent runs were disappointing, even if allowances are made for his wide draws; stall 9 is not ideal today; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D in September but well held twice since; others look stronger

runners.name: Bunta Baby
runners.spotlight: Three C&D wins this year, most recently when running on well to collar Horsepower in this grade in March; not discredited from wide draw here last time, and remains of strong interest.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins this year; not discredited from wide stall last time; remains of interest
runners.owner: Chu Tak Wing, Casey Chu Yin Ling & Ronald Chu Tak Wah

runners.name: Daryl Flash
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over 7f at Sha Tin last month and has now been unplaced all seven Hong Kong starts; can't be recommended from stall 12.
runners.comment: Unplaced all seven Hong Kong starts; overlooked from stall 12

runners.name: Honest Witness
runners.spotlight: Came good with two C&D wins in Class 4 in March/April; flopped badly when upped to this grade last month but further progress remains possible and Zac Purton keeps the faith.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this year; flopped in this grade last time but may yet do better
runners.owner: Arthur Antonio Da Silva, Betty Da Silva & Teresa Da Silva

runners.name: Endurance Express
runners.spotlight: Won over 5f on debut in Australia last year and ran well from the front when fourth on recent Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin (6f); well drawn here; high on the list.
runners.comment: Ran well from the front when fourth on recent Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin

runners.name: Ace Champion
runners.spotlight: 0-7 in Hong Kong but has twice gone close over C&D in recent months; usually ridden patiently; can make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: 0-7 in Hong Kong but has twice gone close over C&D in recent months

runners.name: Triple Fortune
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful handicapper over 7f/1m when based in Ireland, but makes Hong Kong debut after nine-month break and looks opposable over this trip.
runners.comment: Fairly useful over 7f/1m in Ireland; opposable over this trip on Hong Kong debut

runners.name: King Lotus
runners.spotlight: Seemed to have emerged from a dip in form when finishing well for fifth over C&D last month and remains on a workable mark, but others appeal more all the same.
runners.comment: Latest run hinted at possible return to form but others appeal more all the same

runners.name: Amazing Kid
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who has collected four C&D wins this season; still in good form, but not ideally drawn today and the handicapper may have his measure now.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins this season; still in good form but not ideally drawn today

runners.name: The Heir
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 6yo who has won his last two over C&D; can't be ruled out in current mood but combination of rise in grade and wide stall asks a lot of him today.
runners.comment: Won his last two over C&D; up in grade and drawn wide today, though

runners.name: Do You Just
runners.spotlight: Not an instant success in Hong Kong this year but was a clearcut C&D winner in April and his subsequent defeat from stall 12 is not viewed too harshly; may yet have more to offer but has another wide draw today.
runners.comment: Clearcut C&D winner in April and had tough draw when midfield last time

runners.name: Matters Most
runners.spotlight: Reserve; lost his way after scoring at Sha Tin in November, but recent efforts have been perfectly respectable and he's now back on that last winning mark; could have a say.
runners.comment: Recent efforts have been respectable and he's now back on last winning mark

runners.name: Symbol Of Strength
runners.spotlight: Still 3lb higher than for C&D win in March, but was denied a clear run when sixth here three weeks ago and is not badly drawn today; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Denied clear run when sixth here this month; not badly drawn; each-way claims

runners.name: Young Emperor
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent since narrow Sha Tin win in January and had stall 14 when only sixth there last month; much better drawn here and probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Had stall 14 when sixth at Sha Tin last month; much better drawn here; in the mix

runners.name: Jumbo Treasure
runners.spotlight: Made all at Sha Tin in December and was placed off a mark 3lb higher than today's over C&D in March; slightly disappointing on latest outing in April but ideally drawn to attack from the front today; must be considered.
runners.comment: Front-runner; on workable mark and ideally drawn to attack; must be considered

runners.name: Keefy
runners.spotlight: Placed twice here in March (1m1f/1m) and is well handicapped on those efforts, but his form has dipped lately and younger rivals have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Placed twice here in March but form has dipped lately; younger rivals preferred

runners.name: Violet Star
runners.spotlight: Has somewhat mixed record during this first season in Hong Kong, but came from off the pace to score over C&D last month and will presumably be dropped in again from his wide draw today; no surprise if he's on the premises.
runners.comment: Has mixed record in HK but came from off the pace for C&D win last month

runners.name: Another World
runners.spotlight: Won on dirt in early part of this season but has been badly out of form on turf in recent months; needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Badly out of form in recent months

runners.name: Stormi
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row from the front over C&D at start of this year and looked back in prime form when very close second over C&D three weeks ago; can make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins at start of this year; back in prime form this month; bold bid likely

runners.name: Legend Winner
runners.spotlight: Overcame wide draw to open Hong Kong account over C&D two weeks ago, his ninth attempt; much better drawn today but moves up in grade and Zac Purton evidently prefers Romantic Gladiator.
runners.comment: C&D winner this month but up in grade and deserted by Zac Purton today

runners.name: Class
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; yet to make an impact after five Hong Kong starts (7f-1m) and seemingly in need of a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Yet to make an impact after five Hong Kong starts; needs a drop in grade
runners.owner: Jessica Man Yi Lee & Gregory Chi Ho Ho

runners.name: A Americ Te Specso
runners.spotlight: Reserve; ended 2025 with a C&D win off a mark 3lb higher than today's, but this year's form has largely been underwhelming and he's not one to rely upon nowadays.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with a C&D win but this year's form has been underwhelming

runners.name: Romantic Gladiator
runners.spotlight: Game good in Hong Kong at fifth attempt, with C&D success last month; went up 6lb for that but has the inside draw again and Zac Purton keeps the ride; major player.
runners.comment: Off the mark in HK over C&D last month; well drawn again; Purton keeps the ride

runners.name: All Round Winner
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed in this higher grade since Class 4 C&D win in April, but was safely held on last two outings and needs to find hidden reserves today.
runners.comment: Safely held on last two outings; needs to find hidden reserves to overcome this mark
runners.owner: Clement Chan Kam Wing, Maggie Ho Sze Nga Et Al

runners.name: Vecu
runners.spotlight: Quite useful when trained in Britain but well down the field on first two Hong Kong starts (7f/1m).
runners.comment: Well down the field on first two Hong Kong starts

runners.name: Fivefortwo
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who has collected two C&D wins this season and was placed over C&D on last two appearances; not ideally drawn today but still likely to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season and still in good form; likely contender again

runners.name: Mighty Commander
runners.spotlight: In good form over 6f/7f this season (three wins) and stays 1m perfectly well; back on turf after respectable dirt debut this month but current mark demands a career-best performance.
runners.comment: Having a good season over 6f/7f; stays 1m but this mark demands a new career-best

runners.name: Samarkand
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in Class 4 in November and was creditable second to Fivefortwo over C&D in this higher grade on stable debut in March; not seen since, though, and drawn much wider today.
runners.comment: Second over C&D on stable debut in March but not seen since and has a wide draw

runners.name: Definitive
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D last season but has shown better form on dirt this term; needs to prove he can cope with a mark this high on turf.
runners.comment: Won twice over C&D last season but has shown better form on dirt this term
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Kirk Wong King Wai

races.race_name: Prix du Clos de la Barre (Conditions) (Turf)

runners.name: Golden Call
runners.spotlight: Has won nine of his 33 races but a 7yo now and needs to step up on this season's form; finished behind Waldnebel last month.
runners.comment: Has won nine of his 33 races but a 7yo now and needs to step up on this season's form

runners.name: Zakharova
runners.spotlight: Won at two and added a Listed race (1m6f, very soft) last year at Longchamp in September; posted a similar RPR as that day on her reappearance but latest run was underwhelming, trailing home last; possibilities if back to her best.
runners.comment: Latest run was underwhelming but possibilities if back to her best

runners.name: Waldnebel
runners.spotlight: Just one win in 11 starts but some notable form in defeat includes a second to last week's Ascot Gold Cup runner Caballo De Mar in a 1m6f Group 3; good third in a Listed race last time over 1m2f; considered.
runners.comment: Only 1-11 but has some smart form in defeat, including last time out at Listed level

runners.name: Hamavi
runners.spotlight: Listed winner (1m4f, good to soft) who has held his own in a Group 2 and was winning for a fourth time when odds-on for a conditions race at Saint-Cloud in early May (won by 6l; 1m2f, good); every chance.
runners.comment: Listed winner who has held his own in a Group 2; easy odds-on winner last time out

runners.name: Cassius
runners.spotlight: A four-time winner but thoroughly exposed now after 25 races and has an air of vulnerability about him against one or two of these.
runners.comment: Has an air of vulnerability about him against one or two of these

runners.name: Le Combat Continue
runners.spotlight: Only 2-18 but ran a number of good races in defeat last year, including a 2l defeat in a 1m4f Group 2; made an encouraging return when beaten 4.5l in a Listed race over 1m2f, a trip that was on the sharp side.
runners.comment: Ran well in a Group 2 last year; third on return and needs further than that 1m2f
runners.owner: Mme Genevieve Meunier, Elevage Des Vallons Et Al

runners.name: Daryzan
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Daryz; tongue tied for both his races; odds-on when impressing in a newcomers race at Saint-Cloud (1m, soft) and for one so inexperienced he did well to be only 3l off Constitution River in the Group 1 Prix du Jockey Club (C&D, good), especially as he had to be dropped from stall ten; remains a very smart prospect and this looks his to lose.
runners.comment: Hacked up on debut and all things considered he ran very well in the French Derby

runners.name: Iceni Boy
runners.spotlight: Raven's Pass gelding who has completed a hat-trick in 7f/1m races at Prague; it's difficult to get a firm angle on his form but he's clearly talented and an interesting runner from the Czech Republic.
runners.comment: Raven's Pass gelding who has completed a hat-trick in 7f/1m races at Prague

runners.name: Now For Glory
runners.spotlight: Beat a well-bred newcomer with the pair 2l clear in a 1m2f maiden on good to soft at Compiegne; that form has yet to undergo a thorough test but it was a promising start.
runners.comment: Beat a well-bred newcomer with the pair 2l clear in a 1m2f maiden at Compiegne

runners.name: Box Officer
runners.spotlight: Came good in his fifth race, a 1m2f Longchamp handicap over 1m2f a month ago (good to soft); he's in a lot deeper here and has plenty to find.
runners.comment: Recent handicap winner; he's in a lot deeper here and has plenty to find
runners.owner: Ecu Normandy Spirit, Ecu La Vallee Martigny & G Pelisson

runners.name: Gazi
runners.spotlight: Sole win in seven races came over 6f in October (good to soft), since when he's had limitations exposed in two Listed races and two conditions events (7f-1m2f); looks very vulnerable.
runners.comment: Sole win in seven races came over 6f in October; limitations exposed since then

runners.name: Swift Magic
runners.spotlight: Quite strong in the betting for all his races and had a nice bit in hand in a maiden here in May (1m, very soft); holes can be picked in that form but he's looking progressive.
runners.comment: Had a nice bit in hand in a maiden here in May; he's looking progressive

runners.name: Azure Dragon
runners.spotlight: After winning on very soft at Compiegne (1m) he was beaten just over a length in a Listed race at Toulouse (good to soft); he just never gave his running at Saint-Cloud a month ago (1m handicap; 4-1); could be worth another chance.
runners.comment: Close up in a Listed race before simply failing to give his true running last time
runners.owner: Cormac Farrell, William Jackson-Stops & Monaughty Bloodstock

runners.name: Mackaya
runners.spotlight: Took six goes to get off the mark, prevailing narrowly in a 1m maiden at Compiegne (good to soft); unlikely that a repeat of that would see her follow up here.
runners.comment: Took six goes to get off the mark, prevailing narrowly in a 1m maiden at Compiegne

runners.name: Sindalah
runners.spotlight: Both runs at 1m; narrow winner on heavy ground at Deauville first time out and unlikely that she gave her true running when beating one home at Longchamp (good to soft); well-bred daughter of Dubawi who retains potential.
runners.comment: Narrow debut winner and probably failed to give her true running next time

runners.name: Madame Fayette
runners.spotlight: Won over a mile at Lyon Parilly (soft) before proving no match for the odds-on Repel at Nancy (7f); the winner finished well beaten in last week's Sandringham at Royal Ascot; this filly appears to be heading the right way.
runners.comment: Maiden winner; beaten last time but the winner came to Royal Ascot (well held)

runners.name: Kianda
runners.spotlight: Both runs over around 7f at Saint-Cloud; beaten 9l on heavy first time out but then edged home in a tight finish to a maiden on soft; more needed here but that's feasible.
runners.comment: Saint-Cloud maiden winner; more needed here but that's feasible

runners.name: Deal
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never colt with winning form and he had Mister Charmes behind when going down narrowly to a useful rival (who wasn't beaten far in a Listed race the run before) over 1m at Angers (good to soft); others in here have achieved that bit more.
runners.comment: Has winning form; good second last time as well but others in here have achieved more

runners.name: Mister Charmes
runners.spotlight: Won his second race but has just under 2l to find with Deal on their Angers clash earlier this month (1m, good to soft) and there are others to worry about.
runners.comment: Won his second race but has just under 2l to find with Deal on their Angers clash

runners.name: Firefall
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism over the extended 1m1f (AW) here in November, winning easily; down the field since then, with the Group 2 defeat excusable but he was then well beaten in a conditions race; still early days, though.
runners.comment: Impressed on debut but not kicked on this season; it is still early days

runners.name: Afjan
runners.spotlight: 3yo who was second to subsequent King Charles III runner-up Rayevka in a Group 3 at Longchamp (5f, soft) last month and got no run in the closing stages when close fourth of five in Group 3 over this C&D last time (good; Ponntos second); lots to like provided he gets the breaks this time.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Group 3 at Longchamp last month then unlucky fourth in Group 3 here

runners.name: Jawwal
runners.spotlight: Arrives with something to prove having been below par in seventh on his two outings this year; however, he was a close second in the Group 1 Prix de l'Abbaye at Longchamp (5f, very soft) last October and will hold leading claims if rediscovering that form.
runners.comment: Below par in seventh on both starts this year but close second in the Abbaye last October

runners.name: Estepona
runners.spotlight: Went very close in two warm conditions races at Fontainebleu (5.5f, very soft) in March, behind Lesslepasser on the first occasion, and he was denied a clear run for a considerable spell when fifth in Group 3 at Longchamp (5f, soft) last month; interesting, granted better luck.
runners.comment: Denied clear run for considerable spell when fifth in Group 3 at Longchamp last month

runners.name: Mediterannio
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who was progressive last season and continued the good work with a conditions race win here (6f, good) on her reappearance; she's better than she showed when a Listed fifth here (6f, good to soft) last time and it's possible the drop to 5f will be a positive; a possible.
runners.comment: Had been progressive until Listed fifth here last time, but she's better than that

runners.name: Tales Of The Heart
runners.spotlight: Third for Ralph Beckett in Group 3 at Ascot (6f, good to firm) in 2yo campaign in 2024; lightly raced since (including stint in US) and has something to find in today's company but she made a big move to dispute the lead 1f out on last month's stable and seasonal debut here (6f, heavy), before fading into fourth; intriguing contender on this first crack at a bare 5f.
runners.comment: Caught the eye when fourth over 6f here on stable debut; intriguing contender now at 5f

runners.name: Bourgoin
runners.spotlight: 2yo winner at Deauville (6.5f, AW) last December and he's performed respectably among his four starts this year, but needs something extra today.
runners.comment: 2yo win last December; some respectable runs this year but needs something extra today

runners.name: Lesslepasser
runners.spotlight: Beat Estepona by a nose in conditions race at Fontainebleu (5.5f, very soft) on his reappearance; however, he's failed to kick on subsequently and needs to settle better than when last of five over C&D last time.
runners.comment: Won conditions race at Fontainebleu on reappearance but he's failed to kick on since

runners.name: Ponntos
runners.spotlight: Won C&D Group 2 (heavy) in 2024 and this 8yo has won two 5f Listed races this year, in the UAE (AW) and Italy (good); runner-up in Group 3 over C&D (good) latest and this front-runner can make a bold bid despite 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Group 3 over C&D last month and this front-runner can make another bold bid

races.race_name: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Zaahir
runners.spotlight: Ten-time winner, most recently on AW (8.5f) in December; also suited by turf but needs to up his game after some disappointing recent efforts.
runners.comment: Ten-time winner; needs to up his game after some disappointing recent efforts

runners.name: Golden Trip
runners.spotlight: Has modest 1-29 strike-rate but was placed on Polytrack here (9.5f) in April and also ran well when close sixth over that C&D last month; should also be suited by this trip-and-surface combination; high on the list.
runners.comment: Has modest strike-rate but returns to turf after two decent AW runs; high on the list

runners.name: Rubis Gold
runners.spotlight: Must bounce back from a poor run a fortnight ago and has not won since 2024 but was placed off today's mark on consecutive 1m turf starts in April/May and has obvious claims if judged on those efforts.
runners.comment: Ran poorly last time but was placed off this mark on consecutive starts beforehand

runners.name: Weekend Warrior
runners.spotlight: Just 2-46 and was behind Rubis Gold when sixth at Compiegne (1m, heavy) last month but won this race off a slightly higher mark a year ago (good ground) and could be dangerous if perked up by recent stable switch.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2025; could be dangerous if perked up by recent stable switch

runners.name: Showboated
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at 81-1 odds on AW here (1m) in March but quickly back in the doldrums when back on turf last month; others look much safer.
runners.comment: Second at big odds on AW in March but quickly back in the doldrums on next outing

runners.name: Kasimska
runners.spotlight: Regressive mare who has shown very little at triple-digit odds on her recent outings and usually runs over much longer trips.
runners.comment: Regressive mare who has shown very little at triple-digit odds on her recent outings

runners.name: Rosa Trezy
runners.spotlight: Seemed to run well when third of five on AW in Belgium (10.5f) last summer but has been out of form this year.
runners.comment: Out of form this year and not easily recommended

runners.name: Valento
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 7.5f AW win but is difficult to enthuse over after three down-the-field turf runs this spring.
runners.comment: Difficult to enthuse over after three down-the-field turf runs this spring

runners.name: Kavakney
runners.spotlight: Ran poorly at Compiegne a fortnight ago and remains without a win since September 2024 but his staying-on third at Saint-Cloud (1m, good to soft) in April makes him one of the more interesting candidates in this line-up.
runners.comment: Flopped last time but earlier Saint-Cloud third brings him into the equation

runners.name: Rainier
runners.spotlight: 20-race maiden who struggled to get competitive on his last two appearances (both over 1m on soft ground).
runners.comment: 20-race maiden who struggled to get competitive on his last two appearances

runners.name: Gardol Moon
runners.spotlight: Won on Polytrack here (1m) in October; absent since finishing well from the rear for sixth on that surface in December (7.5f) but this return to turf won't be a problem; claims if ready to roll.
runners.comment: AW winner in October; off since October but will be fine back on turf; a possible
